briefdescription.para: Default callback for the driver does nothing.

detaileddescription.para: It matches the signature of the XTmrCtr_Handler type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the callback's data.
is the ID of the timer counter which a user-defined callback would normally operate on.

briefdescription.para: This function populates the timer counter's configuration structure and sets some configurations defaults.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is a pointer to the configuration structure that will be used to copy the settings from.
is the device base address in the virtual memory space. If the address translation is not used, then the physical address is passed.

simplesect.para: Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: (Re-)initializes all timer counters which aren't started already.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if at least one timer counter is stopped.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize fields of the XTmrCtr structure, then reset the timer/counter.If a timer is already running then it is not initialized.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the base address of the device

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has already been started
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the device doesn't exist

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Starts the specified timer counter of the device such that it starts running.

detaileddescription.para: The timer counter is reset before it is started and the reset value is loaded into the timer counter.

If interrupt mode is specified in the options, it is necessary for the caller to connect the interrupt handler of the timer/counter to the interrupt source, typically an interrupt controller, and enable the interrupt within the interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

briefdescription.para: Stops the timer counter by disabling it.

detaileddescription.para: It is the callers' responsibility to disconnect the interrupt handler of the timer_counter from the interrupt source, typically an interrupt controller, and disable the interrupt within the interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

briefdescription.para: Get the current value of the specified timer counter.

detaileddescription.para: The timer counter may be either incrementing or decrementing based upon the current mode of operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: The current value for the timer counter.

briefdescription.para: Set the reset value for the specified timer counter.

detaileddescription.para: This is the value that is loaded into the timer counter when it is reset. This value is also loaded when the timer counter is started.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).
contains the value to be used to reset the timer counter.

briefdescription.para: Returns the timer counter value that was captured the last time the external capture input was asserted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: The current capture value for the indicated timer counter.

briefdescription.para: Resets the specified timer counter of the device.

detaileddescription.para: A reset causes the timer counter to set it's value to the reset value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

briefdescription.para: Checks if the specified timer counter of the device has expired.

detaileddescription.para: In capture mode, expired is defined as a capture occurred. In compare mode, expired is defined as the timer counter rolled over/under for up/down counting.
When interrupts are enabled, the expiration causes an interrupt. This function is typically used to poll a timer counter to determine when it has expired.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: TRUE if the timer has expired, and FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Configures timers to generate PWM output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the period of pwm signal in nano seconds.
is the high time of pwm signal in nano seconds.

simplesect.para: the duty cycle that will possibly be achieved.
This function needs to be called before enabling PWM otherwise the output of PWM may be indeterminate. Here Down count mode of timers are used for generating PWM output.

briefdescription.para: Enables the PWM output as per configurations set by XTmrCtr_PwmConfigure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.

simplesect.para: Call to this function disables only the PWM output and do not alter any configuration. PWM output can again be enabled by calling XTmrCtr_PwmEnable without the need of re-configuration.
